title: Agentic property-management assistant
oneLiner: Agent that handles tenant requests, schedules maintenance, and escalates edge cases to a human — built into an existing PM platform.
problem: Property managers were drowning in routine tenant requests, most of which followed a handful of repeatable patterns but still needed human judgment on the edge cases.
approach: Single-agent architecture with 7 tools (tenant lookup, work-order creation, vendor scheduling, notification send, invoice lookup, lease read, escalation). Eval set built from 200 real historical tickets, graded for routing accuracy and tone.
outcome: Deployed to production. Handles the majority of inbound tenant requests without human touch; edge cases route cleanly to the on-call property manager.
role: Sole engineer — architecture, agent design, evals, deployment.

title: Field-ops dispatch copilot
oneLiner: Copilot that reads incoming maintenance tickets, drafts dispatch plans, and surfaces the right technician for the job.
problem: Dispatchers spent most of their shift pattern-matching tickets to technicians — a job they did well but couldn't scale as the operation grew.
approach: LLM-driven classification + a retrieval layer over technician skills, certifications, and current workload. Copilot drafts the dispatch; the human approves or edits. Full eval harness to catch regressions as the ticket taxonomy evolved.
outcome: Shipped to the dispatch team. Dispatchers now approve ~80% of drafts with minor edits, freeing time for the hard calls.
role: Sole engineer — agent design, retrieval layer, eval harness, production rollout.

title: Agentic sales-call analysis
oneLiner: System that ingests sales-call recordings, scores them against a coaching rubric, and surfaces coachable moments to the rep's manager.
problem: Sales managers couldn't listen to every call their reps made. Off-the-shelf call analysis tools scored calls generically and missed the things this team actually cared about.
approach: Transcript pipeline → rubric-driven LLM judge with per-category sub-prompts → manager dashboard. LLM-as-judge backed by a human-labeled calibration set that I re-ran against every prompt change.
outcome: Production system. Managers use it weekly in coaching. Reduced off-the-shelf tool spend by cancelling the incumbent.
role: Sole engineer — transcript pipeline, LLM judge, dashboard, calibration loop.

title: Construction-ops intelligence platform
oneLiner: Platform that pulls from project management, scheduling, and field-report sources, then answers operator questions in natural language.
problem: Operations leads were stitching together six tools to answer basic questions about project status. Every answer required a human to cross-reference data that already existed.
approach: Unified data layer over existing sources, retrieval-augmented agent on top. Operators ask questions in plain English; agent cites the source document for every answer.
outcome: Live platform. Operators get answers in seconds instead of hours. Full citation trail on every response — non-negotiable for the team's audit requirements.
role: Sole engineer — data layer, agent architecture, citation system.

title: Agentic exam-prep tutor with PDF chat
oneLiner: Tutor that ingests a student's textbook or lecture PDFs and drills them on the material, adapting to what they get wrong.
problem: Students studying for professional exams needed adaptive practice on their own course material, not generic question banks.
approach: PDF ingestion + chunking + retrieval. Tutor agent with three tools (question generation, answer grading, spaced-repetition scheduling). Session memory to avoid re-asking mastered material.
outcome: Shipped as a paid product. Students use it daily; retention metrics outperformed the team's generic-question-bank benchmark.
role: Sole engineer — ingestion, agent, UI, spaced-repetition scheduler.
